availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-phase
availableCommands.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-todo
availableCommands.description: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:insert-phase
availableCommands.description: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:join-discord
availableCommands.description: Join the GSD Discord community (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:verify-work
availableCommands.description: Validate built features through conversational UAT (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:debug
availableCommands.description: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:update
availableCommands.description: Update GSD to latest version with changelog display (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:help
availableCommands.description: Show available GSD commands and usage guide (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:progress
availableCommands.description: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:resume-work
availableCommands.description: Resume work from previous session with full context restoration (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:execute-phase
availableCommands.description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-project
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:set-profile
availableCommands.description: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:map-codebase
availableCommands.description: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents (user)

input.hint: [optional: specific area to map, e.g., 'api' or 'auth']

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
availableCommands.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:settings
availableCommands.description: Configure GSD workflow toggles and model profile (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:audit-milestone
availableCommands.description: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:research-phase
availableCommands.description: Research how to implement a phase (standalone - usually use /gsd:plan-phase instead) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:discuss-phase
availableCommands.description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:remove-phase
availableCommands.description: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-phase
availableCommands.description: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:quick
availableCommands.description: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:cleanup
availableCommands.description: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:health
availableCommands.description: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:reapply-patches
availableCommands.description: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:complete-milestone
availableCommands.description: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:pause-work
availableCommands.description: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:check-todos
availableCommands.description: List pending todos and select one to work on (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions
availableCommands.description: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-milestone
availableCommands.description: Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements (user)

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show current context usage

availableCommands.name: heapdump
availableCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

availableCommands.name: init
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

availableCommands.name: pr-comments
availableCommands.description: Get comments from a GitHub pull request

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: Review a pull request

availableCommands.name: security-review
availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

availableCommands.name: extra-usage
availableCommands.description: Configure extra usage to keep working when limits are hit

availableCommands.name: insights
availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

toolResponse.stdout:
commit 0b2a4fff7f033b9ebb36edffae547c6a70259de1
Author: DonutsDelivery <megusta52@proton.me>
Date:   Tue Mar 10 06:09:02 2026 +0100

    fix: session discovery bugs — SQLite leak, stat race, legacy path encoding
    
    1. SQLite connection leak: db.close() not called when prepare/all throws.
       Wrapped in try-finally with safe close to prevent resource exhaustion.
    
    2. Race condition: stat() before readFile() meant mtime/size could
       mismatch actual content read. Swapped order so stat reflects at
       least the content we got. Added parse error counting with warning
       when >1 line fails (suggests corruption, not just truncated write).
    
    3. Legacy path encoding: Claude changed its encoding scheme — old format
       has no leading dash and preserves spaces. Added encodeProjectPathLegacy()
       and multi-directory scanning with sessionId deduplication to find
       sessions from both old and new encoding schemes.
    
    Task: @bug-discovery
    Spec: @session-discovery
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 .beads/issues.jsonl           |   2 +-
 src/main/session-discovery.ts | 169 +++++++++++++++++++++++++-----------------
 2 files changed, 101 insertions(+), 70 deletions(-)

data.rawOutput:
1→import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'fs'
     2→import { readdir, readFile, stat } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join, isAbsolute } from 'path'
     4→import { homedir } from 'os'
     5→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires
     6→const Database = require('better-sqlite3')
     7→
     8→export interface DiscoveredSession {
     9→  sessionId: string
    10→  slug: string
    11→  lastModified: number
    12→  cwd: string
    13→  fileSize: number
    14→}
    15→
    16→export type SessionBackend = 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    17→
    18→// Message types that indicate actual conversation content (not just summaries)
    19→const CONVERSATION_TYPES = ['user', 'assistant']
    20→const OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME = '.opencode.json'
    21→
    22→function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
    23→  // Claude's current encoding scheme:
    24→  // 1. Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    25→  // 2. Replace / and \ with -
    26→  // 3. Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    27→  // 4. Replace _ with -
    28→  // 5. Replace spaces with -
    29→  // 6. Replace dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30→  return projectPath
    31→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    32→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    33→    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    34→    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    35→    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    36→    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    37→}
    38→
    39→// Claude used a different encoding in older versions (no leading dash, spaces preserved)
    40→function encodeProjectPathLegacy(projectPath: string): string {
    41→  return projectPath
    42→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')
    43→    .replace(/^[/\\]/, '')   // Strip leading slash (no leading dash)
    44→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')
    45→    .replace(/:/g, '-')
    46→    .replace(/_/g, '-')
    47→    .replace(/\./g, '-')
    48→    // Spaces are NOT replaced in the legacy format
    49→}
    50→
    51→function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    52→  if (targetPath === '~') {
    53→    return homedir()
    54→  }
    55→  if (targetPath.startsWith('~/') || targetPath.startsWith('~\\')) {
    56→    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))
    57→  }
    58→  return targetPath
    59→}
    60→
    61→function loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath: string): { data?: { directory?: string } } | null {
    62→  const home = homedir()
    63→  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME
    64→  const candidatePaths = [
    65→    join(projectPath, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    66→    ...(xdgConfig ? [join(xdgConfig, 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)] : []),
    67→    join(home, '.config', 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    68→    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    69→  ]
    70→
    71→  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {
    72→    if (!existsSync(configPath)) continue
    73→    try {
    74→      const content = readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')
    75→      return JSON.parse(content)
    76→    } catch (e) {
    77→      console.error('Failed to parse OpenCode config:', e)
    78→    }
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  return null
    82→}
    83→
    84→function resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath: string): string {
    85→  const config = loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath)
    86→  const configured = config?.data?.directory?.trim()
    87→  const dataDir = configured && configured.length > 0 ? configured : '.opencode'
    88→  const expanded = expandHomePath(dataDir)
    89→  return isAbsolute(expanded) ? expanded : join(projectPath, expanded)
    90→}
    91→
    92→async function discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath: string): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
    93→  const claudeDir = join(homedir(), '.claude', 'projects')
    94→
    95→  // Check both current and legacy encoding schemes to find all sessions
    96→  const candidateDirs = [
    97→    join(claudeDir, encodeProjectPath(projectPath)),
    98→    join(claudeDir, encodeProjectPathLegacy(projectPath))
    99→  ]
   100→  // Deduplicate (they may produce the same result for paths without spaces)
   101→  const uniqueDirs = [...new Set(candidateDirs)].filter(dir => existsSync(dir))
   102→
   103→  if (uniqueDirs.length === 0) {
   104→    return []
   105→  }
   106→
   107→  const sessions = new Map<string, DiscoveredSession>()
   108→
   109→  for (const projectSessionsDir of uniqueDirs) {
   110→    try {
   111→      const files = await readdir(projectSessionsDir)
   112→
   113→      // Process files in parallel for better performance
   114→      const results = await Promise.all(
   115→        files
   116→          .filter(file => file.endsWith('.jsonl') && !file.startsWith('agent-'))
   117→          .map(async (file) => {
   118→            const sessionId = file.replace('.jsonl', '')
   119→            const filePath = join(projectSessionsDir, file)
   120→
   121→            try {
   122→              // Read content first, then stat — ensures mtime/size reflect
   123→              // at least the content we read (file may grow from active writes)
   124→              const content = await readFile(filePath, 'utf-8')
   125→              const fileStat = await stat(filePath)
   126→              const lines = content.split('\n').filter(line => line.trim())
   127→
   128→              // Look for slug and check if session has actual conversation
   129→              let slug = sessionId.slice(0, 8)
   130→              let cwd = projectPath
   131→              let hasConversation = false
   132→              let parseErrors = 0
   133→
   134→              for (const line of lines) {
   135→                try {
   136→                  const data = JSON.parse(line)
   137→                  if (data.slug) slug = data.slug
   138→                  if (data.cwd) cwd = data.cwd
   139→                  if (data.type && CONVERSATION_TYPES.includes(data.type)) {
   140→                    hasConversation = true
   141→                  }
   142→                } catch {
   143→                  parseErrors++
   144→                }
   145→              }
   146→
   147→              // Log if many lines failed to parse (suggests file corruption,
   148→              // not just a single truncated line from a concurrent write)
   149→              if (parseErrors > 1) {
   150→                console.warn(`Session ${file}: ${parseErrors}/${lines.length} lines failed to parse`)
   151→              }
   152→
   153→              if (!hasConversation) {
   154→                return null
   155→              }
   156→
   157→              return {
   158→                sessionId,
   159→                slug,
   160→                lastModified: fileStat.mtimeMs,
   161→                cwd,
   162→                fileSize: fileStat.size
   163→              }
   164→            } catch (e) {
   165→              console.error(`Failed to parse session ${file}:`, e)
   166→              return null
   167→            }
   168→          })
   169→      )
   170→
   171→      // Deduplicate by sessionId (same session may appear in both dirs)
   172→      for (const result of results) {
   173→        if (result && !sessions.has(result.sessionId)) {
   174→          sessions.set(result.sessionId, result)
   175→        }
   176→      }
   177→    } catch (e) {
   178→      console.error('Failed to read sessions directory:', e)
   179→    }
   180→  }
   181→
   182→  // Sort by most recent first
   183→  const sorted = [...sessions.values()]
   184→  sorted.sort((a, b) => b.lastModified - a.lastModified)
   185→
   186→  return sorted
   187→}
   188→
   189→function discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath: string): DiscoveredSession[] {
   190→  const dataDir = resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath)
   191→  const dbPath = join(dataDir, 'opencode.db')
   192→
   193→  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
   194→    return []
   195→  }
   196→
   197→  let db: InstanceType<typeof Database> | null = null
   198→  try {
   199→    db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
   200→    const rows = db.prepare(`
   201→      SELECT id, title, updated_at, created_at, message_count
   202→      FROM sessions
   203→      WHERE parent_session_id IS NULL
   204→      ORDER BY updated_at DESC
   205→    `).all() as Array<{ id: string; title: string | null; updated_at: number; created_at: number; message_count: number }>
   206→
   207→    return rows
   208→      .filter(row => (row.message_count ?? 0) > 0)
   209→      .map(row => {
   210→        const lastModifiedSeconds = row.updated_at || row.created_at || 0
   211→        return {
   212→          sessionId: row.id,
   213→          slug: (row.title && row.title.trim()) || row.id.slice(0, 8),
   214→          lastModified: Number(lastModifiedSeconds) * 1000,
   215→          cwd: projectPath,
   216→          fileSize: 0
   217→        }
   218→      })
   219→  } catch (e) {
   220→    console.error('Failed to read OpenCode sessions:', e)
   221→    return []
   222→  } finally {
   223→    try { db?.close() } catch { /* already errored */ }
   224→  }
   225→}
   226→
   227→export async function discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' = 'claude'): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
   228→  if (backend === 'opencode') {
   229→    return discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath)
   230→  }
   231→  return discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath)
   232→}
   233→

content.text:
```
     1→import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'fs'
     2→import { readdir, readFile, stat } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join, isAbsolute } from 'path'
     4→import { homedir } from 'os'
     5→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires
     6→const Database = require('better-sqlite3')
     7→
     8→export interface DiscoveredSession {
     9→  sessionId: string
    10→  slug: string
    11→  lastModified: number
    12→  cwd: string
    13→  fileSize: number
    14→}
    15→
    16→export type SessionBackend = 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    17→
    18→// Message types that indicate actual conversation content (not just summaries)
    19→const CONVERSATION_TYPES = ['user', 'assistant']
    20→const OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME = '.opencode.json'
    21→
    22→function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
    23→  // Claude's current encoding scheme:
    24→  // 1. Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    25→  // 2. Replace / and \ with -
    26→  // 3. Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    27→  // 4. Replace _ with -
    28→  // 5. Replace spaces with -
    29→  // 6. Replace dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30→  return projectPath
    31→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    32→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    33→    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    34→    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    35→    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    36→    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    37→}
    38→
    39→// Claude used a different encoding in older versions (no leading dash, spaces preserved)
    40→function encodeProjectPathLegacy(projectPath: string): string {
    41→  return projectPath
    42→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')
    43→    .replace(/^[/\\]/, '')   // Strip leading slash (no leading dash)
    44→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')
    45→    .replace(/:/g, '-')
    46→    .replace(/_/g, '-')
    47→    .replace(/\./g, '-')
    48→    // Spaces are NOT replaced in the legacy format
    49→}
    50→
    51→function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    52→  if (targetPath === '~') {
    53→    return homedir()
    54→  }
    55→  if (targetPath.startsWith('~/') || targetPath.startsWith('~\\')) {
    56→    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))
    57→  }
    58→  return targetPath
    59→}
    60→
    61→function loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath: string): { data?: { directory?: string } } | null {
    62→  const home = homedir()
    63→  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME
    64→  const candidatePaths = [
    65→    join(projectPath, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    66→    ...(xdgConfig ? [join(xdgConfig, 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)] : []),
    67→    join(home, '.config', 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    68→    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    69→  ]
    70→
    71→  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {
    72→    if (!existsSync(configPath)) continue
    73→    try {
    74→      const content = readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')
    75→      return JSON.parse(content)
    76→    } catch (e) {
    77→      console.error('Failed to parse OpenCode config:', e)
    78→    }
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  return null
    82→}
    83→
    84→function resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath: string): string {
    85→  const config = loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath)
    86→  const configured = config?.data?.directory?.trim()
    87→  const dataDir = configured && configured.length > 0 ? configured : '.opencode'
    88→  const expanded = expandHomePath(dataDir)
    89→  return isAbsolute(expanded) ? expanded : join(projectPath, expanded)
    90→}
    91→
    92→async function discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath: string): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
    93→  const claudeDir = join(homedir(), '.claude', 'projects')
    94→
    95→  // Check both current and legacy encoding schemes to find all sessions
    96→  const candidateDirs = [
    97→    join(claudeDir, encodeProjectPath(projectPath)),
    98→    join(claudeDir, encodeProjectPathLegacy(projectPath))
    99→  ]
   100→  // Deduplicate (they may produce the same result for paths without spaces)
   101→  const uniqueDirs = [...new Set(candidateDirs)].filter(dir => existsSync(dir))
   102→
   103→  if (uniqueDirs.length === 0) {
   104→    return []
   105→  }
   106→
   107→  const sessions = new Map<string, DiscoveredSession>()
   108→
   109→  for (const projectSessionsDir of uniqueDirs) {
   110→    try {
   111→      const files = await readdir(projectSessionsDir)
   112→
   113→      // Process files in parallel for better performance
   114→      const results = await Promise.all(
   115→        files
   116→          .filter(file => file.endsWith('.jsonl') && !file.startsWith('agent-'))
   117→          .map(async (file) => {
   118→            const sessionId = file.replace('.jsonl', '')
   119→            const filePath = join(projectSessionsDir, file)
   120→
   121→            try {
   122→              // Read content first, then stat — ensures mtime/size reflect
   123→              // at least the content we read (file may grow from active writes)
   124→              const content = await readFile(filePath, 'utf-8')
   125→              const fileStat = await stat(filePath)
   126→              const lines = content.split('\n').filter(line => line.trim())
   127→
   128→              // Look for slug and check if session has actual conversation
   129→              let slug = sessionId.slice(0, 8)
   130→              let cwd = projectPath
   131→              let hasConversation = false
   132→              let parseErrors = 0
   133→
   134→              for (const line of lines) {
   135→                try {
   136→                  const data = JSON.parse(line)
   137→                  if (data.slug) slug = data.slug
   138→                  if (data.cwd) cwd = data.cwd
   139→                  if (data.type && CONVERSATION_TYPES.includes(data.type)) {
   140→                    hasConversation = true
   141→                  }
   142→                } catch {
   143→                  parseErrors++
   144→                }
   145→              }
   146→
   147→              // Log if many lines failed to parse (suggests file corruption,
   148→              // not just a single truncated line from a concurrent write)
   149→              if (parseErrors > 1) {
   150→                console.warn(`Session ${file}: ${parseErrors}/${lines.length} lines failed to parse`)
   151→              }
   152→
   153→              if (!hasConversation) {
   154→                return null
   155→              }
   156→
   157→              return {
   158→                sessionId,
   159→                slug,
   160→                lastModified: fileStat.mtimeMs,
   161→                cwd,
   162→                fileSize: fileStat.size
   163→              }
   164→            } catch (e) {
   165→              console.error(`Failed to parse session ${file}:`, e)
   166→              return null
   167→            }
   168→          })
   169→      )
   170→
   171→      // Deduplicate by sessionId (same session may appear in both dirs)
   172→      for (const result of results) {
   173→        if (result && !sessions.has(result.sessionId)) {
   174→          sessions.set(result.sessionId, result)
   175→        }
   176→      }
   177→    } catch (e) {
   178→      console.error('Failed to read sessions directory:', e)
   179→    }
   180→  }
   181→
   182→  // Sort by most recent first
   183→  const sorted = [...sessions.values()]
   184→  sorted.sort((a, b) => b.lastModified - a.lastModified)
   185→
   186→  return sorted
   187→}
   188→
   189→function discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath: string): DiscoveredSession[] {
   190→  const dataDir = resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath)
   191→  const dbPath = join(dataDir, 'opencode.db')
   192→
   193→  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
   194→    return []
   195→  }
   196→
   197→  let db: InstanceType<typeof Database> | null = null
   198→  try {
   199→    db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
   200→    const rows = db.prepare(`
   201→      SELECT id, title, updated_at, created_at, message_count
   202→      FROM sessions
   203→      WHERE parent_session_id IS NULL
   204→      ORDER BY updated_at DESC
   205→    `).all() as Array<{ id: string; title: string | null; updated_at: number; created_at: number; message_count: number }>
   206→
   207→    return rows
   208→      .filter(row => (row.message_count ?? 0) > 0)
   209→      .map(row => {
   210→        const lastModifiedSeconds = row.updated_at || row.created_at || 0
   211→        return {
   212→          sessionId: row.id,
   213→          slug: (row.title && row.title.trim()) || row.id.slice(0, 8),
   214→          lastModified: Number(lastModifiedSeconds) * 1000,
   215→          cwd: projectPath,
   216→          fileSize: 0
   217→        }
   218→      })
   219→  } catch (e) {
   220→    console.error('Failed to read OpenCode sessions:', e)
   221→    return []
   222→  } finally {
   223→    try { db?.close() } catch { /* already errored */ }
   224→  }
   225→}
   226→
   227→export async function discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' = 'claude'): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
   228→  if (backend === 'opencode') {
   229→    return discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath)
   230→  }
   231→  return discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath)
   232→}
   233→
```
